rationale: In Study 1, the authors explicitly say they apply the Johnson-Neyman technique and cite Spiller et al. (2013) with other methodological sources. They then report Johnson-Neyman points and show regions-of-significance plots, indicating concrete use of that interaction-probing framework in their hypothesis testing. Although grouped with other citations, this is more than a passing mention because it supports an implemented analysis step.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the moderated-regression probing approach associated with Spiller et al. (2013) by using Johnson-Neyman regions of significance to interpret interaction effects. The cited work functions as methodological guidance for simple-effects testing in the paper's regressions.
